meta.name: Tom Yum Soup (Hot & Sour Thai Soup)
meta.subtitle: Bright lemongrass-lime hot & sour broth with galangal, kaffir lime leaf, mushrooms, and shrimp (optional creamy version).

source.name: SSA Thai Catalog
notes: Canonical tom yum for SSA catalogs with both clear (nam sai) and creamy (nam khon) variants.
Uses deterministic ingredient ids and step structure compatible with SSA recipe.schema.json.

summary: Tom yum is Thailand’s iconic hot-and-sour soup: an aromatic broth infused with lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaf, balanced with lime juice, fish sauce, and chili heat—often served with shrimp and mushrooms.

yield.notes: Makes ~1.5–1.7 L soup; serves 4 as a meal with rice or 6 as a starter.

notes: Best fresh. If storing, add lime juice and herbs at reheat time.
Shrimp can overcook on reheat; consider removing shrimp before storing.

tasteProfile.notes: Balance is the goal: fish sauce for salt/umami, lime for sour, chili for heat.

ingredients.label: Chicken stock (or light seafood stock)
ingredients.notes: Water works too; stock adds depth.

ingredients.label: Water (if needed to top up)
ingredients.notes: Use if your stock is very concentrated or to reach desired volume.

ingredients.label: Lemongrass stalks, smashed and cut into 2-inch pieces
notes: Bruise/smash to release oils. These pieces are typically not eaten.

ingredients.label: Galangal, sliced (or ginger as fallback)
notes: Galangal is traditional; ginger is acceptable but different.

ingredients.label: Kaffir lime leaves, torn
ingredients.notes: Tear to release aroma. Remove before eating if desired.

ingredients.label: Thai bird's eye chilies, smashed (adjust to taste)
notes: For mild: use 1 chili or omit and add chili paste at the table.

ingredients.label: Tomato, wedges (optional)
ingredients.notes: Common in many restaurant versions; adds sweetness.

ingredients.label: Shrimp, peeled and deveined
ingredients.notes: Add at the end; cook just until opaque.

ingredients.label: Fish sauce
ingredients.notes: Adjust at the end after lime juice.

ingredients.label: Lime juice (fresh), to finish
ingredients.notes: Add off-heat to keep it bright.

ingredients.label: Roasted chili paste (nam prik pao), optional
ingredients.notes: Adds smoky-sweet depth; common in tom yum goong.

ingredients.label: Evaporated milk (optional creamy version)
ingredients.notes: Traditional creamy tom yum often uses evaporated milk.

ingredients.label: Coconut milk (optional creamy version alternative)
ingredients.notes: Use instead of evaporated milk for a coconut-forward creamy tom yum.

ingredients.label: Chili paste for creamy version (optional)
ingredients.notes: If using evaporated/coconut milk, a little extra chili paste helps maintain flavor intensity.

steps.title: Prep aromatics and add-ins
instructions: Bruise lemongrass with the back of a knife and cut into 2-inch pieces.
Slice galangal; tear kaffir lime leaves; lightly smash garlic and chilies.
Slice mushrooms; slice onion and wedge tomato if using.
Pat shrimp dry and keep chilled until needed.
checks: Aromatics are bruised/torn for maximum infusion.
Shrimp is ready to add at the end (prevents overcooking).

steps.title: Infuse the broth
instructions: Add stock (and water if using) to a pot and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, garlic, and chilies.
Simmer gently to infuse the broth.
checks: Broth smells fragrant and citrusy; keep at a gentle simmer, not a hard boil.

steps.title: Add mushrooms and vegetables
checks: Mushrooms are tender; broth remains clear and aromatic.

steps.title: Cook shrimp gently
instructions: Add shrimp and simmer just until opaque and curled.
Turn off heat as soon as shrimp is cooked through.
checks: Shrimp is just opaque; avoid overcooking.

steps.title: Season and finish (lime + fish sauce)
instructions: Off heat, stir in fish sauce and lime juice.
If using roasted chili paste (nam prik pao), stir it in now for depth.
Taste and balance: add more lime for brightness, fish sauce for salt/umami, or chili paste/chilies for heat.
checks: Hot-sour balance is bright and punchy.
Lime is added off-heat to stay fresh.

steps.title: Serve and garnish
instructions: Garnish with cilantro and green onion if desired.
Optional: remove lemongrass pieces and lime leaves before serving for easier eating.

pairings.notes: Serve with rice to mellow the intensity and stretch the meal.

fridge.notes: Store broth separately if possible; add lime juice and herbs after reheating.

freezer.notes: Not ideal; aromatics dull and shrimp texture suffers. If freezing, freeze broth only.

notes: Do not boil shrimp hard; gentle simmer prevents toughness.
Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.

adaptations.title: Clear (Nam Sai) vs Creamy (Nam Khon)
notes: Clear tom yum: keep as written—lime + fish sauce off-heat.
Creamy tom yum: after shrimp is cooked, stir in 120 ml evaporated milk OR 150 ml coconut milk, plus 1–2 tsp chili paste; warm gently without boiling, then add lime and fish sauce off-heat.

variations.label: Tom Yum Nam Khon (creamy with evaporated milk)

notes: After shrimp is cooked, stir in evaporated milk and a little extra chili paste; warm gently, then finish with lime and fish sauce off-heat.
Use coconut milk instead of evaporated milk for a richer coconut profile; keep heat balanced with extra lime at the end.

ingredient.shrimp.notes: Use 350–450g thin-sliced chicken breast or thigh; simmer 6–10 minutes until cooked, then proceed.

ingredient.fish_sauce.notes: Season to taste; keep the hot-sour balance.

ingredient.stock_chicken.notes: Use a light vegetable stock; avoid overly sweet stocks.

ingredient.shrimp.notes: Add tofu cubes near the end to warm through.

notes: Traditional aromatics (lemongrass/galangal/kaffir lime leaf) are infused then typically left in the bowl; remove for easier eating if desired.
Always add lime juice off heat to prevent bitterness and preserve aroma.
If too sour: add a touch of sugar (not listed) or extra stock; if too salty: add stock/water; if too spicy: add more mushrooms or a splash of coconut milk.

reviewNotes: All ingredients include id+label and amount.value/unit; optional flags used where needed.
All steps include id+title and instructions[] (timers use minutes field).
